parameters.name: tls_ciphers
parameters.description: TLS cipher payload of listener if protocol is terminated_https

parameters.name: allowed_cidrs
parameters.description: allowed cidrs of listener. multiple may be specified separated by comma (1.2.0.0/16,100.0.0.0/8)

parameters.name: listener_protocol
parameters.description: protocol of the listener. May be one of HTTP, HTTPS, SCTP, TCP, TERMINATED_HTTPS or UDP

parameters.name: health_check
parameters.description: health_check will use path to check the health of each backend. If a backend returns a status code of 200-399, then that backend is considered healthy. If it doesn't, the backend is marked as unhealthy for at least health_check_interval and no requests are routed to it. If this option is not provided then health checks are disabled.

parameters.name: health_check_interval
parameters.description: health_check_interval specifies the time between each health check on unhealthy backends. The default interval is 30 seconds ("30s").

parameters.name: health_check_timeout
parameters.description: health_check_timeout sets a deadline for health check requests. If a health check does not respond within health_check_timeout, the health check is considered failed. The default is 60 seconds ("60s").

parameters.name: backends
parameters.description: Provide a list of dictionary with field address and protocol_port. Example: [{'address': 'x.x.x.x', 'protocol_port':'xx'}]

get.summary: Check if the given baremetal (resource_id) is attached to any LB pool member
get.description:
- If attached within the caller's project: returns full attachment details.
- If attached in a different project: returns is_attached=true with a message but no IDs.
- If not attached anywhere: returns is_attached=false.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: limit a number of items (allowed range is 1-100), default is 0.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: a number of items to skip, default is 0.

parameters.name: field
parameters.description: field name to sort the query set, default is id.

parameters.name: ssl_certificate_path
parameters.description: Path to the SSL certificate file

parameters.name: ssl_certificate_key_path
parameters.description: Path to the SSL certificate private key

parameters.name: ssl_certificate_id
parameters.description: CCS ID of SSL certificate files

parameters.name: monitor_name
parameters.description: Display name for the default health monitor created with this virtual server (3–30 characters; required when redirect_https is not enabled).

parameters.name: certificate_id
parameters.description: CCS ID of SSL certificate files

parameters.name: nodes
parameters.description:
Each node must be a dictionary containing the pool member configuration:
                {
                    # Required fields for all nodes
                    'resource_id': str,               # UUID of the compute or baremetal resource
                    'resource_type': str,             # 'compute' or 'baremetal'
                    'resource_ip': str,               # IP address of the resource (validated against port table)
                    'backend_port_id': int,           # ID of the backend port
                    'port': int,                      # Service port number (1-65535)

                    # Optional fields
                    'weight': int,                    # Load balancing weight (default: 1)
                    'max_conn': int,                  # Maximum connections (default: 1000)

                    # Example compute node:
                    {
                        'resource_id': 'uuid-of-compute',
                        'resource_type': 'compute',
                        'resource_ip': '192.168.1.100',
                        'backend_port_id': 5001,
                        'port': 80,
                        'weight': 50,
                        'max_conn': 1000,
                    }

                    # Example baremetal node:
                    {
                        'resource_id': 'uuid-of-baremetal',
                        'resource_type': 'baremetal',
                        'resource_ip': '192.168.1.101',
                        'backend_port_id': 5002,
                        'port': 3306,
                        'weight': 50,
                        'max_conn': 1000
                    }
                }
